finding_results.notes: Roughened all four cited passages. Yusuf (line 115): added self-correction mid-sentence ('is a completely separate —') and a more direct practical conclusion ('You don't need the second to do the first'). Jin-seo's counter (line 119): replaced formal list construction with spoken restart ('On what's actually deployed — you've got legacy builds...') and colloquial phrasing ('They don't come apart'). Petra (line 121): replaced formal endorsement ('Dr. Qadir is correct that') with concessive 'yes,' added hedging dash break ('the contract as written —'), and inserted 'try to' before 'negotiate' as pressure-true qualifier. Jin-seo's closing (line 137): broke composed summary into spoken fragments with dash-hesitation ('So — yes'), pronoun-first then clarification ('They're different things. Emergency access and data rights, those are different problems'), added physical action break mid-speech, and replaced composed literary observation ('I watched what no access looks like in a hospital room') with raw direct statement ('I was in that room'). The emergency-access/data-rights distinction and Jin-seo's motive remain fully legible across all four passages.

summary: Single finding addressed: roughened the four highest-pressure argumentative turns (Yusuf's technical distinction, Jin-seo's counter, Petra's restatement, Jin-seo's closing concession) from thesis-speech into spoken-through delivery using self-correction, dash breaks, hedging, and colloquial restarts. All changes are local to the cited dialogue lines; no structural or continuity impact.
